description: Catalog for removing non-consensual intimate imagery (NCII) and unauthorized redistribution of content you own. Covers: leak-site DMCA notices, search engine intimate-imagery removal, hash registries (StopNCII.org, Meta NCII, NCMEC), platform abuse forms, and jurisdiction-aware legal letter templates. Vanish never stores your content, URLs, or site-list. You run the takedowns yourself — we provide the catalog + templates + audit signing.
important: This catalog addresses a sensitive privacy need. The terminology is deliberately neutral. You are the owner of content you created, the subject of imagery you did not consent to, or a victim of unauthorized redistribution — we do not judge why you need takedown support.

contexts.leak-site: Sites hosting unauthorized reuploads of content (pirated OF, scraped galleries, etc.)
contexts.search-engine: Google / Bing / Yandex — removes the INDEX even if source remains
contexts.hash-registry: StopNCII.org and similar services — platforms scan hashes + block automatically
contexts.platform-report: Direct abuse forms on Reddit / Twitter / Discord / TikTok / Instagram / Telegram
contexts.legal: Letter templates for civil / criminal / cease-and-desist action
contexts.support: Crisis hotlines + non-profit legal aid + trauma-informed resources

jurisdictions.US-federal-DMCA: 17 U.S.C. §512(c) — takedown notice requires sworn statement of copyright ownership and good-faith belief of infringement
jurisdictions.US-federal-SHIELD: Shield Act / 18 U.S.C. §2261A — civil + criminal remedy for non-consensual distribution of intimate imagery
jurisdictions.US-federal-Take-It-Down: Take It Down Act (2025, pending full implementation) — requires 48h removal of NCII including AI-generated
jurisdictions.US-state-revenge-porn: 48 US states have specific NCII laws. Check Cyber Civil Rights Initiative map at cybercivilrights.org
jurisdictions.EU-GDPR: GDPR Article 17 — right to erasure, especially strong for unlawfully processed data
jurisdictions.UK-Online-Safety-Act: UK Online Safety Act 2023 — platforms must remove NCII (including deepfakes since 2024 amendment)
jurisdictions.CA-Criminal-Code: Canada Criminal Code §162.1 — non-consensual distribution of intimate images is a criminal offence
jurisdictions.AU-Online-Safety-Act: Australia Online Safety Act 2021 — eSafety Commissioner can order removal within 24h

coomer.notes: Reposts paid subscription content from OnlyFans/Fansly/Patreon. Frequently changes domain due to takedowns. Multiple mirror sites — if coomer.su returns 404 try coomer.party / coomer.st / current mirror via the official Telegram. The /dmca page is a form, not an email; their backend then forwards to abuse@<currenthost>.
coomer.approach: DMCA to host + Cloudflare + registrar. Cloudflare (abuse@cloudflare.com) often effective for identifying upstream host.

kemono.notes: Sister site to coomer.party, focused on Patreon/Fanbox/similar creator platforms. Same DMCA approach. The /contact page is a web form; same domain-rotation caveat as coomer applies.
kemono.approach: DMCA via their form + escalate to Cloudflare if they host.

thothub.notes: Aggregator of scraped creator content. Responds to valid DMCA notices with proof of ownership.
thothub.approach: DMCA email with: specific URLs + proof of content ownership (OF payout receipt, bank statement, or platform creator dashboard screenshot).

bitchesgirls.notes: Long-running leak site. Slow DMCA response — escalate to hosting provider and search engines simultaneously.
bitchesgirls.approach: Parallel DMCA to site + WebHostingAbuse database lookup + Google de-indexing request.

erome.notes: User-upload adult platform. Responds to DMCA but reuploads are common — hash-based blocking via StopNCII works better long-term.
erome.approach: DMCA for current URLs + StopNCII hash registration for automatic blocking of future uploads.

pornhub.notes: After 2020 reforms, Pornhub requires verified uploaders + has StopNCII hash blocking + responsive DMCA. Also accepts 'content removal requests' without DMCA formality if you're the subject.
pornhub.approach: Content Removal Request form (faster than DMCA for subjects of the content).

xvideos.notes: Responds to DMCA. Has NCII-specific removal form.
xvideos.approach: Use their NCII form (not general DMCA) for intimate content you're the subject of.

telegram-channels.notes: Telegram hosts thousands of NCII channels. Enforcement historically weak but improved post-2023 EU pressure. Report via in-app 'Report Spam' → 'Report as CSAM/NCII' (for 'Child Sexual Abuse Material or Non-Consensual Intimate Imagery').
telegram-channels.approach: Report individual channels via in-app + email abuse@telegram.org. Escalate to eSafety Commissioner (AU) or Ofcom (UK) if hosted there.

discord-servers.notes: Discord's Trust & Safety team responds to NCII reports. Use the specific 'Non-consensual intimate imagery' category in the request form.
discord-servers.approach: Report server to dis.gd/request with 'NCII' category. Provide the server invite link + messages URL if possible.

reddit-subreddits.notes: Reddit has NCII-specific reporting. Subreddits hosting leaked content can be reported + banned.
reddit-subreddits.approach: Report individual posts + the subreddit itself. Reddit's legal at legal-notices@reddit.com for DMCA.

twitter-x.notes: X has a dedicated NCII report form. Faster than general DMCA for subjects of content.
twitter-x.approach: 'Report — Private information or media' form. Attach government ID if requested.

google-intimate.displayName: Google — Remove explicit or intimate personal imagery
google-intimate.notes: Google's DEDICATED form for intimate-imagery removal — faster than general removal. Works even if source site refuses takedown. Request removes image from Google Search + Image Search + Lens.
google-intimate.jurisdictionNotes: Works globally. Google complies within 24-72 hours for verified NCII.
walkthrough: Open support.google.com/websearch/answer/6302812
Scroll to 'Ready to request removal?' → click the link
Sign in to your Google account
Select 'Request removal of explicit or intimate personal imagery'
Enter the Google Search URLs showing the content (one per request)
Upload 1-2 photos of yourself for identity verification
Submit. Google emails you a case reference within 1 hour.

google-results-about-you.displayName: Google — Results about you (general personal info)
google-results-about-you.notes: Complementary to the intimate-imagery form. Monitors for your name/email/phone appearing in ANY results — notifies you of new matches.
walkthrough: Open myactivity.google.com/results-about-you
Set up with your full name + any aliases, address, phone, email
Google scans its index + alerts you when matches appear
You review + approve removal requests one-click

bing-removal.notes: Microsoft covers Bing + integrated searches. Similar to Google's form.
walkthrough: Open bing.com/webmaster/tools/content-removal
Select 'Personal information' → 'Intimate/explicit imagery'
Provide URL(s) + identity verification
Microsoft processes within 72 hours

yandex-removal.notes: Yandex is slower and jurisdiction is Russian (weak enforcement for non-Russian subjects). But it does have a form.
walkthrough: Open yandex.com/support/search/troubleshooting/delete-snippet.html
Submit URLs with identity verification
Specify 'intimate imagery' as category
Response 2-4 weeks

stopncii.operator: Revenge Porn Helpline (UK) + Meta + TikTok + Bumble + Reddit
stopncii.notes: The GOLD STANDARD free NCII tool. You generate hashes of your images LOCALLY (the images never leave your device), upload only the hashes. Participating platforms (Meta, TikTok, Bumble, Reddit, OnlyFans, Pornhub, Snap) scan uploads against the hash registry and auto-block matches. Used by 100K+ victims since 2021.
stopncii.privacyNote: Your actual images never upload — only cryptographic hashes. This is the safest + most effective tool.
walkthrough: Open stopncii.org on a trusted device
Click 'Get Started' → review privacy info
Upload intimate images/videos (processed locally in your browser — they do not leave your device)
StopNCII generates a hash (like a fingerprint) for each
Only the hashes are uploaded to the registry
Participating platforms auto-block matching uploads going forward
You receive a case reference and can request deletion of the hash anytime
stopncii.caveat: Only works on PARTICIPATING platforms (Meta/TikTok/Bumble/Reddit/OnlyFans/Pornhub/Snap). Does not cover aggregator sites like coomer/thothub (which need individual DMCA).

meta-ncii.displayName: Meta NCII Pilot / Not Without My Consent
meta-ncii.notes: Meta's NCII system is now folded into StopNCII.org as of 2022. Submitting via StopNCII automatically covers Facebook + Instagram + Threads + WhatsApp.

ncmec-cybertipline.operator: National Center for Missing & Exploited Children (US, partners internationally)
ncmec-cybertipline.notes: If the content involves anyone under 18 at the time of creation, use CyberTipline — it has mandatory reporting obligations for all US tech platforms and direct law enforcement escalation.
ncmec-cybertipline.jurisdiction: Global (US-based with international partners via INHOPE)
walkthrough: Open report.cybertip.org
File a CyberTipline report — free and anonymous option available
Include URLs of the material + approximate age/date info
NCMEC forwards to ALL affected platforms + FBI + relevant law enforcement globally
You receive a case number for follow-up

dmca-takedown.purpose: Request removal of copyrighted content from hosts that respond to DMCA
dmca-takedown.jurisdictionScope: US law — widely honored globally by hosts/CDNs

cease-and-desist.purpose: Notify an individual (ex-partner, redistributor) to stop
cease-and-desist.template:
To: {{recipientName}}

Subject: Cease and Desist — Unauthorized Distribution of Intimate Imagery

{{recipientName}},

This letter is a formal demand that you immediately CEASE AND DESIST all distribution, posting, sharing, or reposting of intimate imagery depicting me.

Your conduct constitutes:
1. Copyright infringement under 17 U.S.C. §501 (I own the copyright to imagery of myself that I created)
2. {{jurisdictionClause}}
3. Potential civil liability under state revenge-porn / non-consensual-distribution statutes

You are required to:
- Delete all copies in your possession
- Remove all copies you have posted, shared, or forwarded
- Provide written confirmation of deletion within 72 hours of receiving this notice
- Cease any and all further contact regarding me

If you fail to comply, I reserve the right to pursue all available civil and criminal remedies, including but not limited to injunctive relief, monetary damages, and referral to law enforcement.

This letter is being sent {{deliveryMethod}} for evidence purposes.

{{yourName}}
{{today}}

police-report.purpose: Draft language for filing a police report (NCII + revenge-porn laws)
police-report.template:
INCIDENT REPORT DRAFT — NON-CONSENSUAL DISTRIBUTION OF INTIMATE IMAGERY

Reporting person: {{yourName}}
Date of report: {{today}}

INCIDENT SUMMARY
On or about {{incidentDate}}, intimate imagery depicting me was distributed without my consent via the following channels:

{{channelsList}}

The distribution was not authorized by me at any time. I am the subject and, in creating the imagery, the copyright owner of the material.

SUSPECT (IF KNOWN)
{{suspectInfo}}

APPLICABLE LAWS
This conduct may constitute a violation of:
- Federal: 18 U.S.C. §2261A (stalking) and/or Shield Act provisions
- State: {{stateStatute}}
- Take It Down Act (if the content is AI-generated/deepfake)

EVIDENCE PRESERVED
I have preserved screenshots, URLs, timestamps, and audit logs of takedown requests I have filed. These are HMAC-signed via Vanish privacy toolkit and available upon request to investigators.

REQUESTED ACTION
I request that law enforcement:
1. Open an investigation
2. Issue preservation letters to platforms hosting the content
3. Subpoena user data for attribution if the suspect is unknown
4. Refer the matter to the {{federalAgency}} if criminal thresholds are met

Contact information for follow-up:
{{yourContactInfo}}

civil-pre-suit.purpose: Put a platform or individual on formal notice before filing a lawsuit
civil-pre-suit.jurisdictionScope: US — requires modification for other jurisdictions
civil-pre-suit.template:
To: {{recipientName}}

Subject: PRE-SUIT DEMAND — Civil Claims for Non-Consensual Distribution of Intimate Imagery

{{recipientName}},

This letter serves as formal pre-suit notice of my intent to file civil claims against you for the non-consensual distribution of intimate imagery of me.

CLAIMS
1. Public disclosure of private facts (common law tort)
2. Intentional infliction of emotional distress
3. Copyright infringement (17 U.S.C. §501)
4. Violation of {{stateStatute}} (civil NCII remedy, typically $10,000-$150,000 statutory damages)
5. {{jurisdictionClause}}

DAMAGES SOUGHT
- Compensatory damages for emotional distress, lost income, and reputational harm
- Statutory damages per jurisdiction
- Punitive damages
- Attorneys' fees and costs
- Injunctive relief (permanent prohibition on further distribution)

EVIDENCE
I have preserved:
- Screenshots with timestamps of the unauthorized distribution
- HMAC-signed audit logs of takedown notices I have filed
- Copyright registration (if applicable)
- Witness statements

SETTLEMENT OPPORTUNITY
I am willing to discuss settlement prior to filing suit. To open settlement discussions, you must:
1. Confirm complete removal of all copies within 7 days
2. Provide a sworn affidavit of non-redistribution
3. Propose a settlement offer addressing the claims above

If I do not receive a substantive response within 14 days of your receipt of this letter, I will proceed with filing.

Respectfully,
{{yourName}}
{{today}}

ccri-hotline.description: 24/7 crisis hotline for NCII victims. Free legal and emotional support. Can connect you to pro-bono lawyers.

revenge-porn-helpline-uk.description: UK's national helpline. Operator of StopNCII.org. Free, confidential, specialized support.

esafety-commissioner-au.description: Statutory regulator with takedown enforcement power. Can order platforms to remove within 24 hours.

thorn-safer.description: Focused on preventing child sexual abuse material. Works with NCMEC + major platforms.
